# Corine Agent Kit ‚Äî full reference (@corine/core, @corine/cli)

> The safe-by-construction agent runtime for Solana. Every trade goes through ONE guarded execution spine. There is no public path that executes a trade any other way. Bring your own RPC, keys, data and LLM ‚Äî no secret is embedded. TypeScript-first, Apache-2.0, Node >= 18.

This file is the complete, self-contained reference. Everything below is accurate to the shipped API.

## Install

```
npm install @corine/core          # SDK
npm install -g @corine/cli        # CLI
```

## The one idea: the guarded spine

Every trade ‚Äî a one-shot `execute`, a CLI `buy`, or an agent action ‚Äî passes these gates in this order:

1. `kill-switch` ‚Äî global halt; unconditional.
2. `idempotency` ‚Äî a deterministic key can never execute twice; the broadcast signature is recorded before confirmation (no double-send).
3. `mint-sanity` ‚Äî valid, non-identical base58 mints.
4. `per-tx cap` (`maxPerTxUsd`) ‚Äî mandatory.
5. `daily cap` (`dailyCapUsd`) ‚Äî mandatory; the second backstop.
6. `SOL-for-fees` ‚Äî refuse if the wallet can't cover fees.
7. `freshness` ‚Äî refuse on a stale decision (`stale_trigger`) or no live price (`stale_price`).
8. `rug gate` ‚Äî OFF by default; score always computed + recorded; only blocks when enabled.
9. `leg dispatch` ‚Äî the fill venue (`jupiter` by default). Runs ONLY after all gates pass.

`repositionExistingFunds: true` skips gates 4+5 and the daily-spend record (for rebalances/withdrawals of funds already owned); every other gate still applies. This mirrors how the perp leg treats reduce-only closes.

The fill venue is the only pluggable part. A forker implements `ExecutorLeg` and their venue is STILL behind the whole stack. The package does not export a raw `executeSwap`.

## Init

```ts
import { createCorine, LocalSigner } from "@corine/core";

const corine = createCorine({
  rpc: { endpoint: process.env.RPC_URL! },                 // required; or pass an RpcProvider
  signer: LocalSigner.fromBase58(process.env.SECRET_KEY!), // your key, custodial, stays local
});
```

`CorineConfig`: `rpc` (RpcProvider | `{ endpoint, commitment?, cluster? }`, required), `signer?`, `keystore?`, `jupiter?` (`{ restBaseUrl?, swapBaseUrl?, apiKey?, timeoutMs? }`), `prices?`, `quotes?`, `rug?`, `store?` (default `InMemoryStore`), `notifier?` (default `ConsoleNotifier`), `llm?`, `memory?`, `legs?`, `registry?`, `safety?` (Partial<SafetyConfig>).

## Corine API

- `execute(input: ExecuteInput): Promise<GuardedResult>` ‚Äî the ONLY trade method.
- `quote({ inputMint, outputMint, amountUsd, slippageBps? }): Promise<SwapQuote>` ‚Äî read-only preview.
- `price(mint): Promise<number>` ‚Äî live USD, 0 if unavailable.
- `agents: AgentRuntime`.
- `killSwitch: { enable(reason?), disable(), status() }`.
- `store`, `registry`, `rpc`, `cluster`.

`ExecuteInput`: `{ outputMint, inputMint?(=SOL), amountUsd, side?("buy"|"sell"), maxPerTxUsd (required), dailyCapUsd (required), slippageBps?, rugGate?, allowRisky?, repositionExistingFunds?, evaluatedAtMs?, leg?, inputAmountAtomic?, userId?, signer?, idempotencyKey? }`. If `idempotencyKey` is omitted, a 30-second bucketed key is generated (double-submit protection); pass a stable key for retry-safety.

`GuardedResult`: `{ status: "executed"|"blocked"|"failed"|"noop", blockedBy?, reason?, txHash?, fill?, slippageBps?, rugScore?, auditId? }`.

`BlockReason`: `kill_switch | not_whitelisted | over_caps | over_daily_cap | below_min | insufficient_sol | stale_trigger | stale_price | rug | inflight | internal`.

## Example: guarded swap

```ts
const res = await corine.execute({
  inputMint: SOL_MINT, outputMint: USDC_MINT, amountUsd: 10, side: "buy",
  maxPerTxUsd: 100, dailyCapUsd: 500, evaluatedAtMs: Date.now(),
});
res.status === "executed" ? console.log(res.txHash) : console.log(res.blockedBy, res.reason);
```

## The seven interface seams

You supply implementations + your keys; the SDK ships a default for each.

- `RpcProvider` ‚Äî `getBalanceLamports`, `sendRawTransaction`, `confirmSignature`, `getLatestBlockhash`, `cluster`. Default `Web3RpcProvider({ endpoint })`.
- `Signer` ‚Äî `publicKey()`, `signTransaction(tx)`, optional `keypair()`. `Keystore` ‚Äî `getSigner(ref)`. Defaults `LocalSigner`, `SingleKeystore`, `AesKeystore`.
- `PriceSource` ‚Äî `getPriceUsd(mint)` (return 0 when unpriceable). `QuoteSource` ‚Äî `atomicInputForUsd`, `getQuote`. `RugChecker` ‚Äî `checkToken`. Defaults `JupiterClient` (price+quote), `NoopRugChecker`.
- `Store` ‚Äî `isKillSwitchEnabled/setKillSwitch/getKillSwitchReason`, `beginGuard/recordSentSig/completeGuard/failGuard`, `checkDailySpend/recordDailySpend`, `recordAudit`. Defaults `InMemoryStore` (dev), `FileStore` (durable).
- `Notifier` ‚Äî `send(event)`. Defaults `ConsoleNotifier`, `SilentNotifier`.
- `LLMProvider` (optional) ‚Äî `isConfigured()`, `complete(tier, messages, opts?)`. Default `OpenRouterLLM` (bring your key; degrades honestly when absent).
- `MemoryStore` (optional) ‚Äî `remember`, `recall`. Default `InMemoryMemory` (keyword; reports `semanticAvailable:false`).
- `ExecutorLeg` (pluggable fill venue) ‚Äî `{ name, fill(ctx) }`. Default `JupiterLeg`. `ctx.onBroadcast(sig)` must be called on the first signature.

## Agents

Agent types: `DCA | DIP_BUYER | LIMIT_ORDER | LADDERED_EXIT | WALLET_COPY | CUSTOM`. Built-in handlers: `dcaHandler, dipBuyerHandler, limitOrderHandler, ladderedExitHandler` (registered by default). A handler implements `AgentTypeHandler = { type, evaluate(strategy, ctx) => TradeProposal | null }` and may only PROPOSE; the runtime executes proposals through the spine with the agent's caps.

`AgentRuntime`: `deploy({ strategy, userId, walletRef?, state? }) => Agent`, `runOnce(agentId) => GuardedResult | null`, `get/list/pause/resume/kill`. Scheduling is yours ‚Äî call `runOnce` on your cadence; there is no background scheduler.

Strategy (zod, caps MANDATORY): `{ name, agentType, outputMint, inputMint?(=SOL), amountUsd, intervalSeconds?, cron?, priceThresholdUsd?, watchedWallet?, ladder?: {multiplier, sellPercent}[], exitConditions?, caps: { maxPerTxUsd, dailyCapUsd, slippageBps?, rugGate? } }`.

```ts
const agent = await corine.agents.deploy({
  userId: "me",
  strategy: { name: "DCA", agentType: "DCA", outputMint, amountUsd: 5, intervalSeconds: 3600,
              caps: { maxPerTxUsd: 10, dailyCapUsd: 50 } },
});
await corine.agents.runOnce(agent.id); // call on a schedule
```

## Custody & safety honesty

Using `LocalSigner`/`AesKeystore` is **custodial** ‚Äî the process signs on the user's behalf. Never described as "non-custodial"; never say funds are "safe". Caps + kill-switch are backstops on a custodial key. The gates guarantee exactly one thing: you cannot execute a trade that skips them. They do NOT protect against market loss, a compromised key, a dishonest adapter, or non-durability you didn't configure (use `FileStore`/Postgres in production or idempotency + daily caps reset on restart). Full detail: safety-model.md.

## CLI (@corine/cli)

Commands: `quote`, `buy`, `sell`, `price`, `kill on|off|status`, `deploy`, `agents list|run <id>`, `help`. Global flags: `--json` (envelope `{ok, command, data?, error?{code,message}}`), `--yes` (skip confirm; required in `--json` mode for money-moving), `--quote-only` (preview), `--slippage <bps>`, `--in/--out/--usd`. Env: `CORINE_RPC_URL` (required), `CORINE_KEYPAIR` (keyfile path or base58; stays local), `CORINE_MAX_PER_TX_USD` (100), `CORINE_DAILY_CAP_USD` (500), `CORINE_JUPITER_KEY?`, `CORINE_RUG_GATE?`, `CORINE_STATE_FILE?`. Same spine ‚Äî caps + kill-switch apply.

```
corine quote --in So111...112 --out EPjF...Dt1v --usd 10 --json
corine buy   --out <mint> --usd 5 --yes
corine kill on
```

## Not in the open packages

Billing/credits/Dodo, x402, hosted infra (managed DB/queues/API/dashboard/Telegram), and all keys. The runtime never requires billing ‚Äî a trade is never credit-gated. See whats-open.md.

## MCP

Expose `corine_price/quote/buy/sell/kill` to an LLM over the Model Context Protocol; the tools are thin wrappers over the spine, so caps + kill-switch still apply. Reference server: mcp.md.
